2604.28139.title: Claw-Eval-Live: A Live Agent Benchmark for Evolving Real-World Workflows
2604.28139.paper_summary: LLM agents are expected to complete end-to-end units of work across software tools, business services, and local workspaces. Yet many agent benchmarks freeze a curated task set at release time and grade mainly the final response, making it difficult to evaluate agents against evolving workflow demand or verify whether a task was executed. We introduce Claw-Eval-Live, a live benchmark for workflow agents that separates a refreshable signal layer, updated across releases from public workflow-demand signals, from a reproducible, time-stamped release snapshot. Each release is constructed from public workflow-demand signals, with ClawHub Top-500 skills used in the current release, and materialized as controlled tasks with fixed fixtures, services, workspaces, and graders. For grading, Claw-Eval-Live records execution traces, audit logs, service state, and post-run workspace artifacts, using deterministic checks when evidence is sufficient and structured LLM judging only for semantic dimensions. The release contains 105 tasks spanning controlled business services and local workspace repair, and evaluates 13 frontier models under a shared public pass rule. Experiments reveal that reliable workflow automation remains far from solved: the leading model passes only 66.7% of tasks and no model reaches 70%. Failures are structured by task family and execution surface, with HR, management, and multi-system business workflows as persistent bottlenecks and local workspace repair comparatively easier but unsaturated. Leaderboard rank alone is insufficient because models with similar pass rates can diverge in overall completion, and task-level discrimination concentrates in a middle band of tasks. Claw-Eval-Live suggests that workflow-agent evaluation should be grounded twice, in fresh external demand and in verifiable agent action.
2604.28139.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理預期能夠在軟體工具、商業服務和本地工作空間中完成端到端的工作單位。<lb/>然而，許多代理基準在發布時凍結了一組策劃的任務，並主要評分最終回應，這使得評估代理在不斷演變的工作流程需求下的表現變得困難，或驗證任務是否被執行。<lb/>我們介紹了 Claw-Eval-Live，一個針對工作流程代理的即時基準，它將可更新的信號層與從公共工作流程需求信號中更新的內容分開，並與可重現的、時間戳的發布快照相結合。<lb/>每個發布都是根據公共工作流程需求信號構建的，當前發布中使用了 ClawHub 前500 名技能，並具體化為具有固定裝置、服務、工作空間和評分者的受控任務。<lb/>在評分方面，Claw-Eval-Live 記錄執行痕跡、審計日誌、服務狀態和運行後的工作空間產物，當證據充足時使用確定性檢查，並僅在語義維度上進行結構化的 LLM 評判。<lb/>該發布包含 105 個任務，涵蓋受控商業服務和本地工作空間修復，並在共享的公共通過規則下評估 13 個前沿模型。<lb/>實驗顯示，可靠的工作流程自動化仍然遠未解決：領先模型僅通過 66.7% 的任務，且沒有模型達到 70%。<lb/>失敗是根據任務家族和執行表面結構化的，HR、管理和多系統商業工作流程作為持續的瓶頸，而本地工作空間修復相對較容易但尚未飽和。<lb/>僅依賴排行榜排名是不夠的，因為具有相似通過率的模型在整體完成度上可能會有所不同，且任務級別的區分集中在中間帶的任務上。<lb/>Claw-Eval-Live 建議工作流程代理的評估應該在新鮮的外部需求和可驗證的代理行動中進行雙重基礎。
2604.28139.author: Chenxin Li et.al.
2604.28139.authors: Chenxin Li, Zhengyang Tang, Huangxin Lin, Yunlong Lin, Shijue Huang, Shengyuan Liu, Bowen Ye, Rang Li, Lei Li, Benyou Wang, Yixuan Yuan
